feed.title: Google Cloud: Secure Web Proxy
feed.subtitle: Changelog for Google Cloud product: Secure Web Proxy

entry.content:
You can now use authorization policies to perform identity-based and content-based access control checks when processing outbound traffic requests through Secure Web Proxy.

By configuring authorization policies, you can set rules for your workloads to access external destinations. You can also use these authorization policies to delegate complex authorization decisions to identity and content-scanning services like Service Extensions. This feature is supported in Preview.
entry.summary: Secure Web Proxy now supports authorization policies for identity-based and content-based access control on outbound traffic. Configure these policies to manage workload access to external destinations and delegate complex decisions to services like Service Extensions. This feature is available in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: New access control for outbound traffic

entry.content:
You can integrate frontend mutual TLS (mTLS) with Secure Web Proxy to boost the security of your applications and workloads.

With this integration, you can use validated client identities in Secure Web Proxy authorization policies to enforce granular access control for outbound traffic. This feature is supported in Preview.
entry.summary: You can now integrate frontend mutual TLS (mTLS) with Secure Web Proxy to strengthen your application security. This allows for validated client identities in authorization policies, enabling granular access control for outbound traffic. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Enhance Application Security with Frontend mTLS

entry.content:
When deploying your Secure Web Proxy instance as next hop, you can now configure the gateway to listen on all ports (from 1 to 65535). By using this feature, your proxy can automatically intercept and enforce security policies and rules to all outbound traffic, removing the need to manage specific port lists.

This feature is supported in Preview.
entry.summary: Secure Web Proxy now supports listening on all ports (1-65535) when deployed as a next hop. This allows for automatic interception and policy enforcement on all outbound traffic without managing specific port lists. This feature is currently in Preview.
entry.title: Feature: Secure Web Proxy listens on all ports

entry.summary: VPC Service Controls is now generally available. This feature enhances security by allowing you to define a security perimeter around your Google Cloud resources.
entry.title: Feature: VPC Service Controls Now Generally Available
